data.title: Rolls-Royce said to be eyeing funding options including IPO for small nuclear reactor unit
data.description:
Rolls-Royce (OTCPK:RYCEY) has conducted early discussions with advisors to evaluate financing options, including a potential IPO for its small nuclear business, The Financial Times reported on Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Future funding requirements for the business dominated the talks it held with investment houses and banks, two people familiar with the situation said. “There is a live debate within the shareholder base,” one of them added.

The discussions followed the U.K.’s Labour government’s decision to select the London-based industrial giant to construct Britain’s first small modular reactors (SMR) in June. The SMR consortium, majority owned by Rolls-Royce (OTCPK:RYCEF), is currently in discussions with the government to secure a contract later this year.

Qatar Investment Authority and BNF Resources represent other members of the consortium, which also includes CEZ Group, a Czech Republic-based energy company holding a 20% stake as part of a broader collaboration with Rolls-Royce (OTCPK:RYCEF).

The company said it was “not planning for, or in the process of launching, an initial public offering.” This summer, Rolls-Royce (OTCPK:RYCEF) announced that its SMR business, which employs about 800 people, is expected to be free cash flow positive and profitable by 2030.

However, it is not expected to decide on whether to opt for an IPO until it secures the government contract, as the deal’s commercial details could impact valuation.

data.title: Trump’s Tariffs: Key announcements from August
data.description:
Ever since U.S. President Donald Trump has taken office, one of the many things that constantly dominated headlines was his tariff policy.

Although Trump had imposed tariffs during his first term, his latest actions roiled markets, disrupted diplomatic ties, and shook industries. The back-and-forth tariff stance and retaliatory measures have put investors on shaky ground.

Click here to see the tariff timeline from January this year. Here are the key developments that took place in August:

Aug 5, 2025: President Trump said he is prepared to increase the pharmaceuticals tariffs up to 250%, much higher than the 200% he indicated just a few weeks ago.

Aug 6, 2025: President Trump issued an executive order that ratchets up tariffs on goods from India by an additional 25% as a broader measure aimed against Russia. The new tariff to be added to the 25% tariff the Trump administration published last week.

Aug 11, 2025: President Trump has reportedly signed a last-minute executive order extending a pause on high U.S. tariffs on Chinese goods for 90 more days.

Aug 12, 2025: Trump slammed Goldman Sachs and its top boss David Solomon for what he said was a "bad prediction" on tariffs and their effect on the stock market. He asserted that tariffs "have not caused inflation" and said Solomon should "get himself a new economist."

Aug 14, 2025: India and China reportedly discussed the resumption of border trade in locally made goods after more than five years, amid rising trade disputes with the United States.

Aug 20, 2025: Trump administration added 407 product categories to the list of “derivative” steel and aluminum goods covered by Section 232 tariffs, making their steel and aluminum content subject to a 50% duty.

Aug 21, 2025: The U.S. and EU released new details of their trade framework, with key points including the U.S. committing to “apply the higher of either the U.S. Most Favored Nation (MFN) tariff rate or a tariff rate of 15 percent, comprised of the MFN tariff and a reciprocal tariff, on originating goods of the European Union.”

The U.S. and EU also agreed to a conditional 15% tariff on European autos and auto parts entering the U.S., contingent on Brussels passing legislation to lower its industrial tariffs. The White House indicated that tariffs on European pharmaceutical products will be capped at 15%.

Trump also discussed trade with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney, with an agreement to reconvene in the near future.

Aug 22, 2025: India and Russia committed to deepen bilateral trade ties. Both countries set a bilateral trade target of $100B by 2030.

Aug 23, 2025: Trump said imported furniture will be subject to tariffs once an ongoing investigation into the issue concludes within the next 50 days.

Aug 24, 2025: European nations paused shipping packages to U.S. over tariffs.

Aug 26, 2025: President Trump threatened to charge China "200% tariffs or something" if Beijing restricts exports of rare earth magnets to the U.S. Meanwhile, Taiwan and Japan also halted small parcel shipments to the U.S. in response to the Trump administration’s move to scrap the “de minimis” duty exemption for low-value imports.

Aug 27, 2025: President Trump's punitive 25% tariffs on Indian goods took effect. China's President Xi Jinping said he will host more than 20 world leaders convening for a regional security forum in show of solidarity among countries being targeted by U.S. trade policies.

The U.S. Department of Commerce also affirmed anti-dumping and countervailing duties against 10 countries, including Australia, Brazil, Canada, Mexico, the Netherlands, South Africa, Taiwan, Turkey, the UAE, and Vietnam, following investigations into imports of corrosion-resistant steel products.

Aug 29, 2025: The U.S. tariff exemption for package shipments valued under $800 ended permanently on Friday, as the Trump administration formally ends the so-called "de minimis" exemption.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

More on tariff

  • What does the end of 'de minimis' mean for investors
  • Trump’s Tariffs: Key announcements from July
  • Trump admin outlines rare earth plan to break China's dominance
  • Trump says 'we're stopping wars because of tariffs'

data.title: Crypto names IREN, Hut 8 among gainers: Accelerant tanks in week's financial movers
data.description:
Among the week's biggest stock moves for financial names, crypto names appeared among both the biggest gainers and the largest decliners in the week ended Aug. 29, 2025.

IREN Limited (NASDAQ:IREN) topped the list as the bitcoin miner and data center operator soared 24% after its fiscal Q4 adjusted EBITDA topped consensus, and it said it's approaching $1.25B total annualized revenue.

SPAC Cohen Circle Acquisition I (CCIR), which acquired Ukrainian telecom company Kyivstar Group earlier in the month, climbed 22%.

Cipher Mining (NASDAQ:CIFR), another bitcoin miner and data center company, jumped 20%.

Hut 8 (NASDAQ:HUT), also a bitcoin miner and operator of infrastructure, saw its stock bounce up 14% during the week. It announced plans for four new sites, and a deal it's participating in with a company backed by Eric Trump and Donald Trump, Jr., progressed closer to completion.

Miami International Holdings (MIAX), which operates markets for options, futures, and cash equities, rose 12% during the week, about two weeks after the company's IPO.

The week's decliners were led down by Accelerant Holdings (NYSE:ARX), a risk exchange that connects underwriters of specialty insurance risk with risk capital providers, sank 33% after its Q2 revenue missed the consensus estimate. The company debuted in the public markets in July.

Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR), a blockchain technology company that invests in ethereum (ETH-USD), slid 19% after the company boosted its ether holdings by 33%  in a week. In the last five days, the price of ether fell 9.3%. Also during the week, Cathie Wood's Ark Invest bought another $15.6M shares of Bitmine.

The next three decliners are Argentinean banks, which often move based on currency exchange moves. Grupo Financiero Galicia (GGAL) fell 13% in the same week it reported Q2 earnings.

Banco Macro, also based in Argentina, slipped 12% after it reported Q2 results.

Banco BBVA Argentina (BBAR) dropped 11%. It reported Q2 earnings in the previous week.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: AMD, Eli Lilly and Constellation Brands among top picks
data.description:
The S&P500 (SP500) closed in the red on Friday after the week saw key in-line inflation data and major tech stocks, including Nvidia, Dell, and Marvell Technology, decline following quarterly earnings reports.

For the week, Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gained marginally, while Dow (DJI) rose 0.6%.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

AMD upgraded on data center, AI demand

Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) was upgraded to Buy from Hold by Truist Securities due in part to elevated data center and artificial intelligence demand.

Truist also increased its price target on AMD to $213 from $173, saying that some AI hyperscale customers are now seriously considering the switch from Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) to AMD for processing power.

"For the last several years, our industry contacts (component buyers/sellers) have told us that hyperscale customers deploying AI were experimenting with AMD's technology as a 'price check' to NVDA, nothing more," said Truist analyst William Stein.

American Eagle downgraded on tough current operating environment

BofA downgraded American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) to Underperform from Neutral, as it believes the company is a long way from its earnings normalizing in the current environment.

The brokerage, which assigned a PT of $10 on the stock, believes the namesake brand of the company and its sub-brand Aerie are in tough positions to navigate tariffs.

“We think a sales recovery for both brands following the 1Q assortment issues will take time,” BofA said.

Eli Lilly upgraded after latest trial win for obesity pill

HSBC upgraded Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) to Hold from Reduce, noting the latest Phase 3 trial success of the U.S. pharma giant's once-daily weight loss pill orforglipron, developed with Chugai (OTCPK:CHGCF) (OTCPK:CHGCY).

Following a head-to-head analysis between orforglipron and Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) oral diabetes medication Rybelsus, HSBC argued that ATTAIN-2 “in diabesity appears to offer a clear commercial path for filing and commercialization.” “On balance, the orfo profile seems competitive to Rybelsus in diabesity,” the analysts added.

BofA downgrades Constellation Brands on long-term alcohol consumption concerns

Bank of America downgraded Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ) to Underperform from Neutral, saying that the bearish view is based on the potential for further downside as beer industry consumption remains soft and pressures sales, margins, and trading multiples.

Analyst Peter Galbo also warned that long-term alcohol consumption trends show no signs of improving soon and the core Hispanic demographic remains pressured. BofA assigned a price objective of $150 on the stock.

BofA Securities has upgraded Vir Biotechnology (NASDAQ:VIR) to Buy from Neutral saying that the market is underestimating the company's phase 3 combo treatment for severe liver disease caused hepatitis delta virus. The brokerage raised its PT to $14 from $12

Prologis (NYSE:PLD) was upgraded at Scotiabank to Sector Perform from Sector Underperform and increased the price target to $114 from $100 on positive takeaways from the industrial REIT's second-quarter results, minimal downside risk to earnings estimates, and a fairly priced valuation.

Citi initiated coverage on Lumentum Holdings (NASDAQ:LITE) with a Buy rating, expecting more upside in the stock, beyond its current 60.17% rise on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Goldman Sachs COO Waldron sells over $13.6M worth of company stock
data.description:
• John Waldron, President and Chief Operating Officer of Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS), has disclosed the sale of more than $13.6M worth of company stock in a series of transactions this week, a regulatory filing indicated on Friday.
  • Waldron, who also serves as a member of the U.S. banking giant’s board, sold 18,244 shares of Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) on Wednesday and Thursday, according to the SEC Form 4 filing he has submitted.
  • The transactions, conducted at prices ranging from $748.61 to $750.74, have reduced his stake in the company by 15%, as he continues to hold nearly 106.3K Goldman (NYSE:GS) shares per the filing.
  • Waldron, widely considered as CEO David Solomon’s successor, joined the company’s board in February. Both executives were offered stock awards worth $80M each early this year as retention bonuses.

data.title: Cytokinetics posts detailed Phase 3 data for lead asset in hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
data.description:
During a medical event on Saturday, Cytokinetics (NASDAQ:CYTK) announced primary results from its Phase 3 MAPLE-HCM trial for lead asset aficamten in symptomatic obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (oHCM), a condition characterized by abnormally thickened heart muscle.

The trial, designed to test the cardiac myosin inhibitor against the standard-of-care beta blocker metoprolol, reached its main goal related to change in peak oxygen uptake (pVO2), the company announced in May, citing its topline data.

MAPLE-HCM’s detailed data, as presented at a European medical event, indicate that patients on aficamten witnessed an average +1.1 mL/kg/min change in pVO2 from baseline to Week 24 compared to -1.2 mL/kg/min in those who received metoprolol.

Compared to Cytokinetics’ (NASDAQ:CYTK) SEQUOIA-HCM pivotal Phase 3 trial, based on which the FDA is currently reviewing its marketing application for aficamten for HCM, MAPLE-HCM involved those with less severe oHCM.

“These results demonstrate that aficamten meaningfully improves exercise capacity in patients with obstructive HCM, while treatment with metoprolol resulted in a meaningful reduction in exercise capacity,” said CYTK’s R&D head, Fady Malik.

As for safety, the company said that the rates of adverse events were found to be similar across the treatment groups, and a 69-year-old female with multiple health conditions who received aficamten died following a viral disease.

An investor webcast is scheduled for Sept. 2 at 8:30 AM ET to discuss MAPLE-HCM data and other results disclosed at the European Society of Cardiology Congress 2025.

data.title: Trump says “we have to be strong” after appeals court ruling on tariffs
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump took to social media late Friday to announce his displeasure over an appeals court ruling that struck down many historic tariffs he implemented early this year after finding that he overstepped his presidential authority to impose the levies.

“All tariffs are still in effect!” Trump wrote shortly after the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit, in a 7-4 decision, upheld a lower court ruling, noting that a decades-old law that the president invoked doesn’t authorize him to declare the trade deficit a national emergency as a justification for the tariffs.

“If these Tariffs ever went away, it would be a total disaster for the Country. It would make us financially weak, and we have to be strong,” Trump added, suggesting that his administration will appeal the ruling.

The ruling voided Trump’s “Liberation Day” tariffs that set a 10% baseline tariff on nearly all U.S. trading partners and his so-called “reciprocal” tariffs on countries against which the U.S. had trade deficits.

However, the court said that the ruling will not take effect until October 14, giving the White House time to file an appeal with the U.S. Supreme Court.

“If allowed to stand, this Decision would literally destroy the United States of America,” Trump said. “Now, with the help of the United States Supreme Court, we will use them to the benefit of our Nation, and Make America Rich, Strong, and Powerful Again!”

The case didn’t focus on other Trump tariffs imposed on steel, aluminum, and autos as part of Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962, nor on certain China-specific tariffs he imposed during his first term.

On Social media, Attorney General Pam Bondi announced that the administration will appeal the ruling.

data.title: AI is disrupting every industry. These 58 companies are at the top of the list.
data.description:
Artificial intelligence is disrupting every industry, while the valuations on some companies — such as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) — explode higher based on the realities of the spending boom.

And while the benefits of AI have been distributed unevenly so far, investment firm Macquarie believes that any talk of a “bubble” is very premature. 

“We believe agentic AI is poised to become an important battleground between the AI-native disruptors, hyperscalers, and incumbent SaaS vendors,” the investment firm wrote in a note to clients. “However, we think the competition is likely to evolve more slowly than some investors expect, as customers struggle with data readiness, trust/ reliability, and security considerations as precursors to deploying production systems.”

As such, the company put together a list of “AI-native” software companies that are private. The total valuation of the 58 companies is a single-digit percentage of the public software market cap, at $741B (compared to $9.2T), easing concerns about any AI-induced bubble, the analysts added.

Of the 58 companies, the largest is OpenAI, which accounts for roughly 41% of the total AI-native valuation. That's similar to Microsoft, which accounts for 41% of the public software market cap.

And while the majority of revenue from AI-native companies comes from non-agentic AI (large language models accounting for a good chunk of it), adoption of AI agents is exploding and likely to change the narrative.

Here is Macquarie's list of the 58 largest AI-native companies and what they're known for.

OpenAI (ChatGPT, access to large language models)

Databricks (data intelligence)

xAI (Grok)

data.title: Meet Unitree Robotics: The star of the first-ever World Humanoid Robot Games
data.description:
The inaugural World Humanoid Robot Games were held in Beijing earlier in the month. The event featured athletic challenges (such as track, football, and boxing), cognitive tests, and artistic performances, all designed to measure the agility, intelligence, and human-like traits of next-generation humanoid robots. 

While the event showed the challenges of humanoid robotics in some viral moments, Unitree Robotics impressed industry watchers and largely dominated the headlines for its consistent performance in sporting events and dance. The company won 11 medals, including four golds. Its flagship H1 humanoid robot set records in the 400-meter dash, 1,500-meter race, 100-meter hurdles, and the 4×100-meter relay.

Unitree Robotics was founded in 2016 by Wang Xingxing, a robotics engineer whose early work focused on developing affordable and agile legged robots, starting with the XDog prototype during his graduate studies at Shanghai University. The company quickly gained attention for its quadruped robots and its stated goal to offer advanced robotics at a fraction of the cost of Western competitors.

 

 

Looking ahead, by 2026, Unitree expects a significant leap in humanoid robot performance, especially in perception, comprehension, and ability to perform useful tasks. The goal is for general-purpose robots that can serve across domains, not just in demonstrations or limited scenarios.

Unitree Robotics' largest shareholder is founder Wang Xingxing. Meituan (OTCPK:MPNGF) is the second-largest shareholder with approximately 8.24%. Notable investors also include the state-owned Beijing Robot Industry Fund and Shoucheng Holdings. Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY), Alibaba (BABA), ByteDance (BDNCE), Ant Group, Geely (OTCPK:GELYF), and China Mobile all participated in the most recent Series C round as key investors. Notably, Unitree Robotics began the early process for a potential initial public offering in China earlier this year.

The main players in humanoid robotics include Unitree Robotics, Tesla (TSLA) Figure AI (BMW is an investor), Boston Dynamics (majority owned by Hyundai (HYMTF)), Agility Robotics, Apptronik (Google is an investor), Engineered Arts, Sanctuary AI, Fourier Intelligence, PAL Robotics, Hanson Robotics, Toyota Research Institute (TM), Shadow Robot Company, Beijing HRIC, EngineAI, Kepler, and Agibot.

Related humanoid robotics ETFs: Roundhill Humanoid Robotics ETF (BATS:HUMN), KraneShares Global Humanoid & Embodied Intelligence Index ETF (NASDAQ:KOID), Themes Humanoid Robotics ETF (NASDAQ:BOTT), Global X Robotics & Artificial Intelligence ETF (NASDAQ:BOTZ), and ROBO Global Robotics & Automation Index ETF (ROBO).

data.title: Sanctioned Russian LNG cargo delivered to China for first time, defying U.S.
data.description:
China this week received liquefied natural gas cargo from a sanctioned Russian project for the first time, according to ship-tracking data from Kpler, reflecting China's continued willingness to import Russian LNG despite Western sanctions.

The Arctic Mulan vessel reportedly discharged the LNG cargo it was carrying from Russia's Arctic LNG 2 export plant at China's Beihai terminal, marking the first successful delivery from the project, which has faced difficulties in finding buyers after it was blacklisted by the Biden administration in late 2023.

"Arctic LNG 2 has shipped 13 cargoes since beginning exports but has not [previously] delivered any cargoes due to extensive Western sanctions on Arctic LNG 2 and the so-called 'dark fleet' of vessels used to lift its cargoes," Kpler's Camille Klass wrote.

The tanker's delivery came as Russia's President Putin is due to arrive in Beijing this Sunday to meet with China's President Xi and other leaders.

"China and Russia are testing the waters," Anne-Sophie Corbeau of Columbia University's Center on Global Energy Policy said in a post on LinkedIn.

If the delivery is completed without any U.S. reaction, it "could be a signal for China and other buyers that it would be OK to buy sanctioned Arctic LNG 2 cargoes," Corbeau wrote. "India could be next in line for a Russian LNG cargo, especially at an attractive price."

Crude oil futures fell Friday but scored modest gains in a week marked by speculation over how much India could reduce its Russian oil purchases, fading expectations for Russia-Ukraine peace talks, and a second straight draw in U.S. crude stockpiles.

U.S. crude production hit a record high in June, rising 133K bbl/day to 13.58M bbl/day, and natural gas production in the lower 48 states also edged higher in June to reach a record 120.7B cf/day, the Energy Information Administration reported Friday.

On the demand side, product supplied of crude and petroleum products in the U.S. - considered a proxy for demand - jumped 684K bbl/day in June to 21M bbl/day, its highest since October 2024, gasoline demand increased 205K bbl/day to 9.23M bbl/day, its highest since July 2024, and jet fuel demand rose 84K bbl/day to 1.85M bbl/day, its highest since August 2018.

Money managers have reduced their bullish position on U.S. crude to the lowest in more than 18 years, the Commodity Futures Trading Commission reported this week, on concerns that the oil market is headed for a supply surplus near the end of the year and that President Trump's trade war ultimately will hurt energy consumption.

Net-long bets on WTI crude were cut by 5,461 lots to 24,225 lots in the week ended August 26, the lowest since January 2007, and short-only bets against WTI rose to a 20-month high, the CFTC data showed.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for October delivery finished the week +0.5% to $64.01/bbl, and front-month Brent October crude (CO1:COM) closed the week +0.6% to $68.12/bbl; on Friday, WTI and Brent fell 0.9% and 0.7%, respectively.

U.S. natural gas (NG1:COM) posted solid weekly gains with help from firming LNG feedgas flows and a below-average inventory build that reduced the storage surplus over the five-year average to its lowest level since early June; front-month Nymex October natgas rose 1.8% on Friday and settled +7% this week at $2.997/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (NYSEARCA:XLE)

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), finished the week +2.5%.

Top 10 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Uni-Fuels Holdings (UFG) +31.2%, Green Plains (GPRE) +24.4%, Energy Vault (NRGV) +23.2%, American Resources (AREC) +15.7%, Eco Wave Power (WAVE) +15.4%, Knot Offshore Partners (KNOP) +14.6%, Delek US Holdings (DK) +13.2%, Vital Energy (VTLE) +12.8%, PBF Energy (PBF) +12.3%, CVR Energy (CVI) +11.7%.

Top 5 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Piedmont Lithium (PLL) -21.3%, Vivopower International (VVPR) -14.7%, Maxeon Solar Technologies (MAXN) -13.1%, Canadian Solar (CSIQ) -11.6%, Empire Petroleum (EP) -9.8%.

Source: Barchart.com
